items.toString: In the shared folder, the collection channel folder for the dev fix environment has not been created.
Payment Partner export file - external documents, rescheduling, LPFs

items.fromString:
*Reproduce Steps:*

We have a collection channel for Payment Partner → [https://testapps.energo-pro.bg/app/phoenix-epres/collection-channel/preview?id=1008|https://testapps.energo-pro.bg/app/phoenix-epres/collection-channel/preview?id=1008]

*Actual Result:*

\\dfs-p01-fs-01.bulgaria.eon.net\bg_epro_team$\140\collection_channel_test\collection-channel\EasyPay

Example export file:  [^Epay_export_20251212_080000.txt] 

1)For liabilities transferred from KVASY, we have the Liability[id], but it should contain the value from the outgoing document from the external system. Also, the document date is empty — it should be taken from the Occurrence Date field.



!image-20251212-084119.png|width=674,alt="image-20251212-084119.png"!

!image-20251212-084232.png|width=674,alt="image-20251212-084232.png"!

 2)For LPFs, the document date is also empty — it should be taken from the Logical Date / Occurrence Date field.

!image-20251212-084901.png|width=674,alt="image-20251212-084901.png"!

# For the rescheduling, the positions of the subsequent columns have shifted, which is a problem:
!image-20251212-090428.png|width=537,alt="image-20251212-090428.png"!



_________________

fields.summary: Payment Partner export file - external documents, rescheduling, LPFs

content.text: We have a collection channel for Payment Partner →

1)For liabilities transferred from KVASY, we have the Liability[id], but it should contain the value from the outgoing document from the external system. Also, the document date is empty — it should be taken from the Occurrence Date field.
2)For LPFs, the document date is also empty — it should be taken from the Logical Date field.
For the rescheduling, the positions of the subsequent columns have shifted, which is a problem:
Hello, we fixed some issues related to this bug, but we have identified additional ones. The current TXT file structure for the payment partner does not work correctly in some cases:

Based on the documentation, the invoice prefix + invoice number can only be 12 digits in total. However, for manual liabilities, the system should take the number from the “Outgoing document from an external system” field, and this value can be 12 digits. According to the documentation, it is unclear how this data should be inserted into the file.
The rescheduling number can be more than 12 digits, and when it exceeds this length, the rescheduling number is not displayed correctly in the file.
According to the documentation, we only have 2 columns for the invoice prefix, but some prefixes can contain more than 2 digits. Because of this, the invoice number start position is shifted.
maybe we need some changes for this cases ?

Hello, the prefix length should be 3 characters (the following columns should be shifted by one position). When we have an outgoing document from the external system, the prefix should be “000”. For the example described in this issue, the prefix + invoice number should be “0000222357185”.
When we have a rescheduling liability, the prefix should be “RES” + the rescheduling ID with leading zeros. For example, Rescheduling-1065 should be “RES0000001065”.

status.description: This status is managed internally by Jira Software
status.name: Ready for testing
